title: provider-account-decoupling data contracts

Family.description: Canonical provider family identifier. MUST be in Account.knownFamilies(). Examples: "codex", "openai", "anthropic", "gemini-cli".

AccountId.description: Opaque per-account identifier. Today happens to be `<family>-(api|subscription)-<slug>`; treat as opaque outside Account.generateId. MUST NEVER appear as providerId outside accounts.json storage keys.

ModelVector.description: Three-dimensional address used by rotation, identity audit, and persistence.

AuthBlob.description: Auth credentials returned by Auth.get(family, accountId).

ProviderInfo.description: Family-level provider entry in the registry. Per-account state lives under `accounts`.

accounts.description: Per-account state for this family. Key MUST be a valid AccountId; value carries auth options + cooldown state.

AccountsJson.description: On-disk shape of ~/.config/opencode/accounts.json. STRUCTURE UNCHANGED by this refactor; only invariant is tightened.

MigrationMarker.description: Marker file `.migration-state.json` written by the migration script and verified at daemon boot.

Errors.description: New error types introduced by this refactor.

RegistryShapeError.description: Thrown when a non-family providerId is inserted into the providers map.

UnknownFamilyError.description: Thrown when Auth.get / getSDK receives a family not in knownFamilies.

NoActiveAccountError.description: Thrown when Auth.get(family) is called without an accountId AND the family has no activeAccount set.

MigrationRequiredError.description: Thrown at daemon boot when `.migration-state.json` is missing or version != "1".

invariants: ProviderInfo.id === the key under which it is stored in providers[]
Every providers[] key is a Family
Every Model.providerId emitted from the registry is a Family
Every accounts.json families[] key is a Family
Auth.get is two-arg (family, accountId?); single-arg form removed
getSDK is three-arg (family, accountId, modelId); object-arg form removed
